name: The Bridges of Malpaso - circular from Molinaseca via the Camino Francés (PR-LE 42)
name_original: Las Puentes de Malpaso – circular desde Molinaseca por el Camino Francés (PR-LE 42)
region_intro: Hiking around Molinaseca offers diverse terrain within the El Bierzo region of León, Spain. The village is situated in a scenic river valley, with the Meruelo River flowing through it, surrounded by mountains. This environment features a mix of rugged mountains, flat orchards, and vineyards, creating a distinct V-shaped valley. The area is characterized by natural features such as the Meruelo Valley and fragrant expanses of heather and broom. Best hiking trails around Molinaseca The most popular hiking route is The Bridges of Malpaso - circular from Molinaseca via the Camino Francés (PR-LE 42), a 6.9 miles (11.2 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 21 minutes to complete. This route connects to the historic Camino Francés and offers views of the river valley. Another top favourite among local hikers is La Mirada Circular Nature Trail, an easy 2.2 miles (3.5 km) path. This nature trail provides an accessible option with gentle elevation gains, typically completed in 57 minutes. Local hikers also love the Molinaseca Town Sign – Boeza Bridge loop from Campo, a 9.1 miles (14.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain with river views, often completed in about 3 hours 59 minutes. Hiking around Molinaseca is defined by river valleys, surrounding mountains, and historical landmarks like the Roman Bridge. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy nature walks to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Molinaseca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 170 reviews. More than 1300 hikers have used komoot to explore Molinaseca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Molinaseca?
A: Molinaseca offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with over 140 routes available. These range from easy strolls to more challenging treks, ensuring options for all skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Molinaseca?
A: Hikes in Molinaseca feature diverse terrain. You'll find paths winding through a scenic river valley, alongside the clear waters of the Meruelo River, and ascending into the surrounding mountains. The landscape includes a mix of rugged mountain areas, flat orchards, and vineyards, often fragrant with heather and broom.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or casual walkers in Molinaseca?
A: Yes, Molinaseca has many easy hiking options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047088747/la-mirada-circular-nature-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mirada Circular Nature Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an accessible 3.5 km path with gentle elevation gains, typically completed in under an hour. There are over 45 easy routes in the area.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced trekkers near Molinaseca?
A: For experienced trekkers seeking a challenge, Molinaseca offers several demanding routes. One such option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38170353" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Montes de León – Cruz de Ferro (Iron Cross) loop from Cruz de Ferro&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 24.8 km trail with significant elevation gain, typically taking over 7 hours to complete. There are 9 difficult routes in the area.

Q: What historical landmarks or natural features can I see while hiking in Molinaseca?
A: Molinaseca's hikes often pass by significant landmarks and natural beauty. You can explore the medieval streets of the village, cross the historic &lt;strong&gt;Molinaseca Roman Bridge&lt;/strong&gt;, or follow sections of the famous &lt;strong&gt;Camino de Santiago&lt;/strong&gt;. Natural features include the picturesque Meruelo River Valley and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5542980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de las Hoyas Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Further afield, the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38170353" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Ferro (Iron Cross)&lt;/a&gt; is a notable monument for pilgrims.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Molinaseca?
A: Yes, Molinaseca is well-suited for circular hikes. Popular options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041036372/the-bridges-of-malpaso-circular-from-molinaseca-via-the-camino-frances-pr-le-42" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Bridges of Malpaso - circular from Molinaseca via the Camino Francés (PR-LE 42)&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 11.2 km loop, and the easier &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047088747/la-mirada-circular-nature-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mirada Circular Nature Trail&lt;/a&gt;. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40311869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molinaseca Town Sign – Boeza Bridge loop from Campo&lt;/a&gt;, which offers river views.

Q: Are Molinaseca's hiking trails suitable for families with children?
A: Many trails around Molinaseca are suitable for families. The region offers numerous easy routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047088747/la-mirada-circular-nature-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mirada Circular Nature Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which are relatively short and have minimal elevation gain, making them ideal for walks with children. Look for routes marked as 'easy' for the most family-friendly experiences.

Q: Are dogs allowed on hiking trails in Molinaseca?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in Molinaseca. The natural environment provides ample space for pets to enjoy. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or near other hikers, and to follow local regulations regarding pet waste.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Molinaseca?
A: Molinaseca is beautiful throughout the year, but spring and autumn are particularly pleasant for hiking. Spring brings lush greenery and blooming wildflowers, while autumn offers vibrant foliage. Summers can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, especially if you plan to enjoy the river beach. Winters are generally mild, but some higher elevation routes might be affected by snow.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Molinaseca?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 170 reviews. Reviewers often praise the picturesque river valleys, the historical charm of Molinaseca village, and the variety of trails that cater to different fitness levels. The connection to the Camino de Santiago and the serene natural environment are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to visit on hikes near Molinaseca?
A: Yes, you can find waterfalls near Molinaseca. A notable natural attraction is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5542980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de las Hoyas Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which can be incorporated into longer hiking excursions. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10461355" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gualtón Waterfall loop from Carracedo de Compludo&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 6.8 km hike.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Molinaseca?
A: Molinaseca, being a popular stop on the Camino de Santiago, offers various parking options within or near the village. Specific parking availability can vary by trailhead, but generally, you'll find designated parking areas close to the main access points for popular routes. It's recommended to check local signage upon arrival.
tips: Nice enclave of the Las Puentes de Malpaso route when we cross the Meruelo river.
Popular trail with good views near the town of Riego de Ambrós, where the French Way of Saint James passes.

The Sanctuary of Nuestra Señora de las Angustias in Molinaseca is closely linked to the Camino de Santiago since it is one of the most important points on the route that connects Astorga with Santo Tomás de las Ollas through the Foncebadón pass.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of Our Lady of Sorrows in Molinaseca
